Scenario.label: Start / Lead-in
Scenario.description: Ensure Calculator is open and in a clean state. Reuses existing instance if found; launches a new one otherwise. Clears any pending expression via RESET.

Lead-in for every calculator scenario. 1. LISTWINDOWS on SYSTEM to find existing calc window. 2. If absent: send SENDKEYS to run calc.exe (or use LAUNCH if available); wait 1 s. LISTWINDOWS again to get the new hwnd. 3. RESET on the window to clear any pending expression. Bind the resolved hwnd to {{hwnd}} for subsequent steps.

Step.note: discover existing calc window; bind hwnd if title contains 'Calculator' or 'Kalkulačka'
launch if absent — sends to Start/Run; skip if window already found

Scenario.label: Compute Expression
Scenario.description: Type an arithmetic expression into Calculator and return the result. Supports basic arithmetic: +, -, *, /. Example: expression="7 * 6" → result "42".

Use this to arithmetically evaluate an expression on the live Calculator window. Steps: 1. intro (reset to clean state) 2. SENDKEYS the expression digits and operators character by character. Operator mapping: + → SENDKEYS +, - → SENDKEYS -, * → SENDKEYS *, / → SENDKEYS /. Digits map directly to numXButton characters. 3. SENDKEYS {ENTER} (or CLICKID equalButton) to evaluate. 4. READ the window to get the result string. The result appears in CalculatorResults; the numeric value in TextContainer. Expression "{{expression}}" — replace placeholder with actual expression string.

Step.note: type expression — digits and operators go directly to the focussed window
read full window text including result

Scenario.label: Click-Based Computation
Scenario.description: Compute a simple two-operand arithmetic expression by clicking buttons. Slower than SENDKEYS but immune to locale keyboard-layout differences.

Most reliable method for simple expressions (no floating-point, no parentheses). Steps: 1. intro 2. CLICKID numXButton for each digit of first operand. 3. CLICKID <operatorButton> (plusButton / minusButton / multiplyButton / divideButton). 4. CLICKID numXButton for each digit of second operand. 5. CLICKID equalButton (or SENDKEYS {ENTER}). 6. READ result from window. Use this approach when SENDKEYS key-mapping is uncertain (non-English locale). Parameters: operand1, operator (one of: plus, minus, multiply, divide), operand2.

Step.note: first digit of operand1 — expand per-digit at runtime
first digit of operand2 — expand per-digit at runtime

Scenario.label: Read Current Display
Scenario.description: Read the current value shown in Calculator without pressing any buttons.
Use when you need to know what is currently on the display without performing any operation. Calls READ on the window; the response contains both the expression string and the result string.

Scenario.label: Teardown: Leave Open
Scenario.description: Leave Calculator open after the test sequence (default teardown).
No-op teardown. Calculator remains open and in the post-sequence state. Use when subsequent tests will reuse the same window (RESET at start of next test).

Scenario.label: Teardown: Reset State
Scenario.description: Reset Calculator state (AC) but leave the window open.
Presses AC/clearButton to leave Calculator at 0 with empty expression. Use to clean up after a test without closing the application. Equivalent to RESET command.

Scenario.label: Teardown: Close App
Scenario.description: Sends Alt+F4 to close the Calculator window. Use only when explicitly required — prefer teardown-leave-open or teardown-reset to avoid slow relaunch.
